join_order.rationale: Customer bitset (188KB) fits L2 cache; zone map skips ~92% of orders blocks since orders are sorted by o_orderdate
Orders hash map (~1.2M × 16B ≈ 20MB) fits in 44MB L3 cache; bloom filter eliminates ~92% of non-matching l_orderkey hash-table probes (15M distinct orderkeys, 1.2M in build set)

logical_plan.aggregation_notes: GROUP BY (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority); since o_orderdate and o_shippriority are fully determined by l_orderkey via the orders join, use l_orderkey as the primary hash key and store {o_orderdate, o_shippriority, revenue_sum} in the slot. Estimated ~50K-150K groups — pre-size hash map at 262144 (next power-of-2 above 150K at 75% load factor). Thread-local hash maps per thread, merged sequentially after parallel phase.

filter_pushdown.implementation: Scan dictionary file customer/c_mktsegment_dict.txt to find code for 'BUILDING'; scan codes column comparing code == BUILDING_CODE
Zone map on orders/o_orderdate_zone.idx: skip blocks where block_min >= 9247; orders are physically sorted by o_orderdate so ~92% of blocks are skipped
Zone map on lineitem/l_shipdate_zone.idx: skip blocks where block_max <= 9247; lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate so early blocks are skipped

topk.rationale: K=10 is tiny relative to group count; heap avoids full O(n log n) sort; O(n log 10) = O(n)

data_structures.customer_filter: bitset(1500001) — 188KB, fits L2 cache; set bit at index c_custkey for each BUILDING customer; O(1) test during orders scan
data_structures.orders_bloom_filter: bloom_filter(~1.5MB for 1.2M keys at 10 bits/key, ~1% false positive rate) — built alongside orders hash map, checked before every lineitem hash map probe to avoid L3 cache misses on non-matching keys
data_structures.orders_join_map: compact_hash_map<int32_t, {int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority}>(pre_sized=2097152) — open-addressing with robin hood or linear probing; 1.2M entries × 12B payload ≈ 20MB, fits within 44MB L3 cache
data_structures.aggregation_local: per_thread compact_hash_map<int32_t, {int64_t revenue_sum, int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority}>(pre_sized=262144) — 64 thread-local maps each ~3MB; merge sequentially post-parallel-scan
data_structures.topk_heap: std::priority_queue (min-heap) of size 10, ordered by (revenue ASC, o_orderdate DESC) to maintain top-10 by revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC

phase_1_customer_filter.rationale: 1.5M rows × 1 column (int16 codes) = 3MB scan; completes in <2ms; bitset build not parallelizable without synchronization and not worth it at this size

phase_2_orders_scan.synchronization: lock_free_cas on orders hash map inserts (1.2M inserts, low contention) OR sequential build after parallel filter pass
phase_2_orders_scan.preferred: two_pass: parallel filter into thread-local position lists, then sequential hash map build from merged lists
phase_2_orders_scan.rationale: Zone map prunes ~92% of 150 blocks; effective scan volume ~1.2M rows across 12-13 blocks; parallel scan minimal benefit here but preserves pipeline uniformity

phase_3_lineitem_scan.synchronization: thread_local_aggregation_maps — no contention during hot loop; sequential merge after parallel phase
phase_3_lineitem_scan.rationale: Dominant phase: ~51M rows scanned after zone pruning (~600 blocks, skip ~90-100 blocks); 64 threads × 100K morsel = full parallelism; thread-local agg maps (~262K pre-sized) avoid all write contention

phase_4_merge_and_topk.rationale: Merge 64 thread-local agg maps into one (~50K-150K total groups); then single-pass heap to find top-10; negligible cost vs scan phases

parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge for aggregation; lock_free_cas for orders hash map build if parallel

index_usage.purpose: Skip orders blocks where block_min >= 9247 (epoch days for 1995-03-15); orders are sorted by o_orderdate so this eliminates ~92% of blocks (only first ~12-13 of 150 blocks need scanning)
Skip lineitem blocks where block_max <= 9247; lineitem sorted by l_shipdate so early blocks (pre-1995-03-15 ship dates) are skipped

scan_details.customer: fused scan+filter+bitset_insert: load c_mktsegment codes, compare to BUILDING_CODE, set bit in bitset[c_custkey[i]]
scan_details.orders: fused zone_prune+scan+filter+bitset_probe+hashmap_insert: for each non-pruned block, load o_orderdate, check < 9247; if pass, load o_custkey, probe bitset; if match, load o_orderkey + o_shippriority and insert into orders hash map
scan_details.lineitem: fused zone_prune+scan+filter+bloom_probe+hashmap_probe+aggregate: for each non-pruned block, load l_shipdate, check > 9247; if pass, load l_orderkey, check bloom filter; if bloom pass, probe orders hash map; if match, load l_extendedprice + l_discount, compute revenue, update thread-local agg map

revenue_computation.notes: All values stored scaled by 100; integer arithmetic throughout; final output divides accumulated sum by 100 for display. No floating-point needed.

physical_plan.memory_notes: Orders hash map (~20MB) fits within 44MB L3 cache — lineitem probe into it will be mostly L3 hits. Customer bitset (188KB) fits L2. Bloom filter (~1.5MB) fits L3. Thread-local agg maps (64 × ~3MB = 192MB total) fit within 376GB RAM but not L3 — this is acceptable since agg updates are sparse (only ~2.4% of scanned lineitem rows match).

target_comparison.notes: Key speedups over current GenDB: (1) zone map on orders skips 92% of scan volume, (2) customer bitset avoids hash map overhead for dimension join, (3) bloom filter avoids ~92% of L3 hash probes on lineitem, (4) thread-local aggregation eliminates synchronization bottleneck, (5) Top-K heap avoids full sort. Target: ~80-100ms, competitive with DuckDB.
